items.caption_text:
Monthly SIP of 41.2 lakhs

From 10000₹ to managing wealth worth 3200 crores. @iamoneidiot has truly come a long way.

Check out his answer

items.caption_text:
Comment “SIP” and I’ll personally DM you the entire conversation.

In this video I’m in conversation with Gajendra Kothari. He is currently investing ₹41 lakhs into SIPs every month.

In this video Gajendra shares his entire journey. He has been investing into mutual funds for the last 20 years. And he shares his learnings, his mindset and why he is now investing ₹41 lakhs into SIP.

He shares how you can create wealth by investing in SIPs. How people on salary can retire and become financially free. It’s all about staying invested and delaying the gratification. He let compounding do it’s work and even for you to see the magic of compounding you should invest in SIP and let it be there for 15-20 years.

Subscribe to my channel for mind bending podcast episodes every Wednesday and Saturday!

items.caption_text:
Buying vs Renting 🏡💸 — which one is really better?

Buying a home means building equity, enjoying stability, and potentially earning from property appreciation. But it also comes with mortgages, taxes, and maintenance costs.

Renting gives you flexibility, lower upfront costs, and less responsibility for repairs. But the money you pay each month doesn’t turn into an asset, and long-term, you miss out on investment growth.

So, should you buy or rent? It depends on your financial goals, lifestyle, and long-term plans. For some, owning is freedom. For others, renting is peace of mind.

What’s your pick? 🤔

#BuyVsRent #HomeDecisions #FinancialFreedom #InvestSmart #RealEstateTips MoneyMatters”

items.caption_text:
Comment SIP to understand why paying tax might actually be your biggest wealth hack.

Most people obsess over saving tax.
Very few focus on growing post-tax money the right way.

In this conversation, Gajendra Kothari, long-term investor, CFA Charterholder, founder of Etica Wealth, shares a counter-intuitive insight -

Paying TAX Makes You RICH! 
Earn ₹100.
Pay tax honestly.
Invest the rest in compounding assets at 15% for 15–20 years.

The result?
Cleaner money.
Bigger compounding.
Zero stress.

He says real wealth isn’t built by hiding income.
It’s built by letting white money compound with discipline.

(SIP investing India, compounding strategy, tax planning mindset, long term equity investing, white money vs black money, Gajendra Kothari, personal finance India, Finance With Sharan)

items.caption_text:
Comment ‘SIP’ and I’ll send the link straight to your DMs.

₹62 crore portfolio.
No house. No car. Came by train. 🚆

That’s Gajendra Kothari for you.

While most people chase returns, he’s chasing discipline.
₹42 lakh SIP per month. Paid ~₹5 crore in taxes last year. Still renting by choice.

No gold rush. No silver FOMO.
Calls both “bubble territory.”

His real edge?
Not diversification like the 99%.
But conviction like the top 1%.

When everyone buys 5-star rated funds,
he buys the last ranked fund if the quality is intact.

True contrarian investing is lonely.
But sometimes, that’s where the alpha is. 📈

Keywords: Gajendra Kothari, contrarian investing, asset allocation strategy, portfolio management, SIP investing, gold bubble, silver returns, fund ranking strategy, wealth mindset, 1 percent thinking

items.caption_text:
In 2026 don’t lose out on personal finance. Start your personal finance journey with this podcast! 🤑💸

In this episode, finance experts Gajendra Kothari (@iamoneidiot), Sanjay Kathuria (@financebysanjay), and Kirttan Shah (@kirtanshahcfp) talk about what real wealth actually means and how it is built over time. They break down how much money is truly “enough,” the role of mutual funds, equities, ETFs, SIP versus lump-sum investing, and why asset allocation matters most during bear markets. The discussion goes beyond numbers into investor psychology, fear cycles, EMI traps, and the importance of having the right financial guidance. They also examine how geopolitics, US tariffs, and RBI’s gold strategy influence markets, explore the future of money through gold, silver, and digital assets, and close with practical advice, book recommendations, and a grounded outlook on the markets ahead making this episode a concise blueprint for anyone serious about long-term wealth creation.

items.caption_text:
In this rare, no-nonsense episode, host Jayesh Gangan sits down with Gajendra Kothari (@iamoneidiot), Sanjay Kathuria (@financebysanjay), and Kirttan Shah (@kirtanshahcfp) for a brutally honest conversation on wealth, markets, and the psychology that decides who gets rich and who doesn’t.

This is not motivational finance. This is how money actually works.

What we unpack in this episode:
- Real Wealth Beyond Numbers and Lifestyle Flexing
- Defining How Much Money Is Truly Enough and Why It Is Personal
- Choosing Between Mutual Funds, Equities and ETFs Based on Investor Profile
- SIP vs Lump Sum Investing During Bear Markets
- Tactical Asset Allocation vs Long Term Investing Discipline
- Understanding Investor Fear Cycles and Panic Behaviour
- The EMI Trap and Its Impact on Long Term Wealth Creation
- Do You Really Need a Financial Advisor or Can You Invest Independently
- How Geopolitics, US Tariffs and Global Shocks Affect Indian Portfolios
- RBI’s Gold Strategy and Its Broader Economic Signals
- The Future of Money Gold, Silver, Copper, Currency and Stablecoins
- Must Read Investing Books and Practical Frameworks for Real World Application
- Market Outlook Ahead Without Hype or Sensational Predictions

This episode is a masterclass in thinking clearly about money especially in uncertain times.

📺 Premieres tonight at 8 PM.
👉 Do you invest with a plan or with hope?

items.caption_text:
Kirtan Shah & Gajendra Kothari on 2026 Investing Guide 🎙️

In this no-hype, data-driven episode of the Sanjay Kathuria Podcast, we bring together Kirtan Shah and Gajendra Kothari — two of India’s most trusted voices in wealth management and long-term investing — to decode exactly how smart investors will build wealth in 2026.

They cut through noise and break down why most investors fail, why SIPs alone aren’t enough, and how professionals use asset cycles, fund selection, and disciplined allocation to create serious wealth.

If you want a clear, practical investing roadmap for stocks, mutual funds, gold & silver — this episode is essential.

💡 What You’ll Learn

✅ Gold vs Equity — what to focus on in 2026
✅ Why SIPs alone won’t make you rich
✅ How to invest lump-sum money correctly
✅ Mutual fund selection rules professionals follow
✅ Sector & factor investing made simple
✅ How to judge a good fund manager
✅ Big AMC vs Small AMC — what really matters
✅ Rules to build a ₹10 Cr portfolio

💬 Comment “Podcast” to Watch Now

🔖 Hashtags

#Investing2026 #MutualFundsIndia #WealthCreation #KirtanShah #GajendraKothari #SIPInvesting #GoldVsEquity #LongTermInvesting #AssetAllocation #SanjayKathuriaPodcast

items.caption_text:
Everyone talks about high-end PMS & AIFs… but hardly anyone knows that mutual funds are the most tax-efficient wealth-building vehicle for everyday investors.

✔️ Zero tax till you redeem
✔️ Lowest cost among all products
✔️ Dividends reinvest tax-free
✔️ No annual tax drag like PMS
✔️ HUGE benefit through Section 54F (buy a house with your MF gains → pay ZERO long-term capital gains tax)

This is how real compounding happens.

@iamoneidiot @vivbajaj

items.caption_text:
Everyone wants high returns with low risk—but that combination doesn’t exist.

The truth?
Investing becomes dangerous only when you don’t know your risk profile.
Stop copying your neighbour’s returns or some expert’s volatility tolerance.
This journey is a single-player game, and your only benchmark is you.

Find your place on the risk scale.
Be honest about your expectations.
And build wealth at your pace—safely and sustainably. 🚀📈 

#stockmarket #indianstockmarket #face2face #stockmarketindia #tradingstrategy #mutualfunds
